All diagnoses are already in hand. This is a pure synthesis task — no further probing needed. Let me produce the report from the data.

# briefer.news Feed-Freshness Triage — 26 Flagged Sources

## Executive summary

The dominant pattern is **systemic, not per-source**: the single largest cluster is the **lean-edition State Dept RSS cohort**, where an intermittent CloudFront/Akamai **soft-200 "Technical Difficulties" page is served only to bare/minimal request shapes** (no User-Agent), parses to 0 items *without* tripping feedparser's `bozo` flag, and is silently absorbed — so 7-9 State Dept feeds went STALE in lockstep ~24d ago. Critically, **two independent verifications proved the production fetch path actually sends the BrieferBot UA and gets the real fresh feed** (Europe & Eurasia: 10 fresh items; Africa: 9 fresh via curl_cffi), which contradicts the "host outage / dead URL" diagnoses and points the real defect at either (a) the prod run not passing HEADERS on this path, or (b) the lean RSS extraction phase itself. The **highest-leverage single finding is the DOJ-Antitrust diagnosis: the lean rss/web_scrape EXTRACTION phase discovered 1643 stubs but extracted 0 on 5/31** (vs 65-141 on good days) — the entire lean rss cohort shares the identical 24d-silent signature, so one extraction-phase bug, not 9 feed bugs, likely explains most of the lean flags. Fix that phase first, then re-evaluate. Separately, only **3 sources are clean mechanical fixes that actually verified** (SPC, State Council Yaowen, Xinhua Leaders, ORNL — 4 verified, one needs a code handler too), and a large tail is **correct low-cadence silence** that should simply be ignore-listed.

---

## Category A — Verified safe fixes (mechanical, low-risk; `verification.verified === true`)

Only fixes that are applyable + were independently confirmed to surface fresh items.

### Root cause: BROKEN_LINK_PATTERN

**Supreme People's Court** (china) — `verified=true`, 14 fresh items
- File: `pipeline/config/china_sources.yaml` line 270 (link_pattern, SPC block)
- Change: `https?://www\.court\.gov\.cn/[a-zA-Z0-9_/]+\.html` → `/[a-zA-Z]+/xiangqing/\d+\.html`
- Why: `_discover_html()` runs `pattern.search()` on the RAW href *before* urljoin; the homepage emits only relative hrefs. Old pattern matched 0/216 anchors; new pattern matched 110 raw hrefs → 90 distinct article URLs (correctly excludes nav/pagination).
- Evidence: sample `https://www.court.gov.cn/zixun/xiangqing/501491.html`, body 发布时间 2026-05-31 14:13:01, 来源 最高人民法院新闻局. 14 articles within the ~14d window. Fully self-contained mechanical edit, no method change.

### Root cause: STALE_FEED_SITE_FRESH (URL-only repoint, no code change)

**Xinhua Politics — Leaders** (china) — `verified=true`, 34 fresh items
- File: `pipeline/config/china_sources.yaml` ~L78 (listing_url, block L75-84)
- Change: replace dead hub `https://www.news.cn/politics/leaders/index.htm` with the per-leader index pages, e.g. `https://www.news.cn/politics/leaders/xijinping/index.htm`, `.../liqiang/index.htm`, `.../zhaoleji/index.htm`, `.../wanghuning/index.htm` (multi-URL).
- Why: hub is a static directory with 0 dated article links; per-leader pages are plain-curl (NOT JS), and the existing `link_pattern` + `extractor=detail` match unchanged.
- Evidence: 34 unique fresh URLs (≥20260518) across just 4 leader pages incl. 5 dated today + 1 dated 20260601. Sample `https://www.news.cn/politics/leaders/20260531/0a91da38a8304f97abe716c09023bac4/c.html` (publish 2026-05-31 11:55, `id="detail"` present).
- Caveat: this is a multi-URL listing swap (handler must accept a list), not literally a one-token edit. The pattern/extractor stay as-is.

**ORNL — Oak Ridge News** (lean) — `verified=true`, 7 fresh items
- File: `pipeline/config/sources.yaml` line 296 (url, block 294-300)
- Change: `https://www.ornl.gov/news` → `https://www.ornl.gov/news/releases`
- Why: the `/news` hub surfaces a static "featured" set (newest May 14, all already in DB); the date-sorted `/news/releases` listing carries the fresh `/news/<slug>` releases. `link_pattern "/news/"`, `extractor playwright` unchanged. Matched nav links are harmless (extractor resolves real dates; main.py sorts by publish_date desc).
- Evidence: sample `https://www.ornl.gov/news/underground-acoustic-signals-reveal-hidden-tunnels` (May 28) + SAMMY/VENUS (May 27); inline `<time>` attrs confirm newest-first ordering. Method-preserving, same host, brand-safe.

### Root cause: STALE_FEED_SITE_FRESH (verified BUT requires a code handler — not a pure YAML edit)

**State Council Yaowen (Top News)** (china) — `verified=true`, 138 fresh items
- Files: `pipeline/config/china_sources.yaml` lines 55-56 **AND** `pipeline/scraper/china_scrape.py` (new handler)
- Change: set `discovery_type: json_list` + `listing_url: https://www.gov.cn/yaowen/liebiao/YAOWENLIEBIAO.json`; keep `link_pattern` (L57) and `extractor: pages_content` (L60). **Add** a `_discover_json_list` handler to china_scrape.py (`_DISCOVERY` map currently has only mfa_press_conf, html_curl_cffi, xinhua_home; `_discover_html` does BeautifulSoup `find_all('a')`, so JSON yields 0 anchors as-is).
- Why: configured listing_url decayed to a JS-redirect stub → AJAX shell; the real list is the JSON feed. 291/400 URLs match the existing pattern; 138 are fresh within 14d.
- Evidence: sample `李强签署国务院令…对外投资的规定` — 2026-06-01 — `https://www.gov.cn/yaowen/liebiao/202606/content_7070766.htm`; pages_content extraction confirmed (~782 chars).
- **Routing note:** the data half verified, but because it needs a new code handler it is **not a no-judgment mechanical edit** — treat as A-with-code-work / borderline B. The verifier explicitly flagged "repoint listing_url alone is INSUFFICIENT."

---

## Category B — Needs your decision

### B1. The lean State Dept RSS cohort — the diagnoses CONTRADICT each other; investigate before any edit

This is the most important block. The per-source diagnoses split three ways and **two verifications overturned their own diagnosis**:

- **State Dept — Europe & Eurasia** (diagnosed DEAD_URL, `verified=FALSE`): the host-outage premise is **FALSE**. With the production UA `Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; BrieferBot/1.0; +https://briefer.news) Feedparser/6.0`, the configured URL returns real RSS, 10 entries, all fresh. The 659KB "Technical Difficulties" page is served ONLY to a bare/no-UA request. The production path (`discovery.py:107 feedparser.parse(url, request_headers=HEADERS)`) already sends a UA.
- **State Dept — Treaties (TIAS)** (diagnosed DEAD_URL, `verified=FALSE`): same correction — UA-filtering, not a dead URL. Feed parses with a browser UA, but newest real item is 2026-05-08 (22d) at ~1-3/month cadence, so it yields nothing fresh most days anyway.
- **State Dept — Africa** (STALE_FEED_SITE_FRESH, `verified=TRUE`): curl_cffi gets 9 fresh items reliably; plain fetch is **intermittently** blocked (3/3 real on a later retry). Confirms the block is real but non-deterministic.
- **State Dept — Near East / East Asia** diagnoses both note the soft-200 page parses to 0 entries **without `bozo`**, so `discovery.py`'s empty-feed guard never fires and a multi-week stall went undetected.

**The contradiction that must be resolved first:** if prod already sends the UA (Europe verification) yet all 9 desks went STALE in lockstep (watchdog), then the failure is **NOT the UA and NOT a dead URL** — it is either (a) the lean run not actually passing HEADERS / a proxy stripping the UA on the scheduled run, or (b) the **extraction phase** (see B2). 

**Recommendation:** Do **NOT** apply the DEAD_URL fixes (no URL change, do not mark active:false, do not treat as outage — that would suppress healthy fresh gov feeds). Instead:
1. Confirm via a verbose scheduled-run log whether HEADERS is actually sent on the lean RSS path and survives any proxy.
2. Harden `discovery.py` fetch_rss (~L112-114): when `feed.entries == []` AND body is HTML / content-type isn't xml-rss, **log a WARNING and retry once** (closes the silent-0-items blind spot regardless of root cause).
3. Add an `Accept: application/rss+xml,...` header (Near East diagnosis) as cheap insurance.
4. Only after the extraction phase (B2) is fixed should you re-judge whether any State Dept feed is genuinely broken.

Affected files: `pipeline/scraper/discovery.py` (HEADERS ~L23-28, fetch_rss ~L112-114). No `sources.yaml` URL edits.

### B2. DOJ — Antitrust Press Room → EXTRACTION_FAILS (highest leverage; pipeline code, not YAML)

- Source config verified healthy live (URL 200, 12 `/opa/` anchors, newest May 21 extracts ~7.5k chars). The defect is the lean rss/web_scrape **extraction phase**: on 5/31 it discovered 1643 stubs but extracted **0** (loop aborted in ~1s; only 4 at midday) vs 65-141 on good days. The **entire** lean rss cohort (BLS, CFPB, ARPA-E, all State Dept, Court of Intl Trade) shares the identical 24d-silent signature.
- **Recommendation:** fix this FIRST — it may explain most lean flags at once. Investigate `pipeline/main.py` rss/web_scrape extraction loop + `pipeline/scraper/extractor.py` (shared Playwright browser dying/hanging early). Re-run the rss phase under verbose logging. Fallback worth adding: httpx extraction for `justice.gov /opa/` pages (body extracts fine via plain curl). Do NOT touch `sources.yaml:270`.

### B3. MIIT News → BROKEN_LINK_PATTERN, fix did NOT verify (`verified=FALSE`, 0 fresh)

- The proposed regex is structurally correct but matches **only the stale 2024-09 snapshot** at the configured listing_url; fresh section indexes (e.g. `/jgsj/index.html`, MakeTime 2026-05-25) inject article anchors **client-side**, empty in curl_cffi HTML. The China `_DISCOVERY` map has no playwright option.
- **Tradeoff:** a real fix requires EITHER adding a playwright discovery function to `china_scrape.py` OR locating the MIIT TRS-WCM JSON list endpoint (all probed candidates 404'd in the read-only session). MEMORY flags MIIT as the one dead China listing with no substitute, so keeping it has editorial value.
- **Recommendation:** route to a dedicated follow-up to find the JSON list endpoint (cheapest if found) or add the playwright path. **Do not apply the regex-only fix** — verified to yield 0 fresh.

### B4. People's Daily Politics → BOT_BLOCKED (hard 403)

- `http://politics.people.com.cn/` hard-403s curl_cffi chrome (RMW-WEB host block), reproduced live; siblings Opinion (d=6) and CPC News (d=41) work, individual politics articles return 200.
- **Tradeoff/recommendation:** (1) try `discovery_type: html_playwright`; (2) find a politics-host listing path returning 200 with `n1` links (`/GB/1024/index.html` was 200 but 0 matching links — needs probing); (3) else drop — CPC News already discovers `politics.people.com.cn/n1/...` under the same pattern (partial overlap). File: `china_sources.yaml` ~L168-177.

### B5. China Electricity Council → BOT_BLOCKED (TCP-level)

- `www.cec.org.cn` (211.160.76.55) refuses TCP from the mini's IP (curl-28 timeout every run); curl_cffi can't help (block precedes TLS). Sibling `huiyi.cec.org.cn` reachable, so domain is alive.
- **Recommendation:** set `active: false` (energy already covered by NEA/NDRC/Stats Bureau) — file `china_sources.yaml` L340-350 — OR escalate to an in-China egress/proxy. No coverage gap from dropping.

### B6. BLS → BOT_BLOCKED + mis-routed config

- The only running BLS source is the lean `type: rss` hitting Akamai-protected `bls_latest.rss` via plain httpx → hard 403 every run (curl_cffi gets 200, but the feed is a single rolling summary item, a poor article source). The correct per-release curl_cffi feeds exist in `akamai_sources.yaml` L301-326 (Employment Situation/CPI/PPI) but appear in **ZERO logs** — configured-but-not-executed.
- **Recommendation:** (a) deactivate `sources.yaml` L198-202; (b) investigate why the akamai BLS entries aren't in the rotation (likely missing DB source-id registration, not active flag). Needs a rotation/registration check.

### B7. State Dept — Western Hemisphere → diagnosed LOW_CADENCE_OK but really a dedup artifact (operator choice)

- Feed is healthy and fresh (newest 29 May), but all bureau feeds share the office-of-the-spokesperson URL pool with the umbrella "State Dept — Press Releases" feed; global `url_hash` dedup (`main.py:118`) credits each release to whichever source inserts first (umbrella wins), so bureau `scraped_at` never advances → false STALE. **Note:** this explanation is in tension with B1/B2 (if the umbrella is ingesting these URLs, the content isn't lost; if the extraction phase is dead, nothing is ingested). Resolve after B2.
- **Recommendation:** once B2 is settled, either add the 7 bureau feeds + Collected Releases to `BRIEFER_FRESHNESS_IGNORE`, or drop them from `sources.yaml` (they only tag bureau metadata on already-ingested releases).

### B8. State Dept — East Asia & Pacific → STALE_FEED_SITE_FRESH, proposed fix did NOT verify (`verified=FALSE`)

- The proposed `link_pattern ^https://www\.state\.gov/[a-z0-9-]{15,}/$` matches **0/10** real articles; corrected pattern `^https://www\.state\.gov/releases/[a-z0-9-]+/\d{4}/\d{2}/[a-z0-9-]+/$` matches 10/10. The `/feed/` fallback is an empty 0-item stub. **Bigger problem:** there is NO server-rendered EAP-filtered endpoint (`?bureaus=` ignored server-side, `/regions/...` 404s) — a listing scrape ingests the entire global press-release firehose with no regional scoping = scope regression + heavy duplication across the 5 sibling regional feeds.
- **Recommendation:** do NOT commit the proposed fix. If the RSS path is salvageable (see B1 — the regional RSS may work fine with the right UA/extraction), keep RSS. Only if RSS is truly dead, accept these collapse into one generic "State Dept Press Releases" source with keyword post-filtering — an editorial decision.

---

## Category C — Genuinely fine (low cadence; `LOW_CADENCE_OK`, not bugs)

These are correct silence. **Add exactly these names to `BRIEFER_FRESHNESS_IGNORE`** (in `launchd/news.briefer.feedfreshness.plist`):

```
ARPA-E — News & Insights, CFPB — Newsroom, State Dept — Diplomatic Security, State Dept — International Organizations, Court of International Trade, Australia DFAT — Departmental Media Releases, Joint Chiefs of Staff
```

Per-source justification (all confirmed publishing-cadence, not defects):
- **ARPA-E** — playwright renders the React SPA fine; newest 2026-04-28; a few items/month. (Optional hardening: ingest `https://arpa-e.energy.gov/jsonapi/custom/index/news` directly — method change, not a fix.)
- **CFPB Newsroom** — RSS healthy, matches human page exactly; ~monthly amid agency wind-down; will tip to DEAD bucket soon purely from cadence.
- **State Dept — Diplomatic Security** — RSS valid on retry; newest 30 Apr; ~monthly. *Caveat:* shares the state.gov soft-block surface (B1) — if B1/B2 reveal these are actually being blocked, re-classify to B.
- **State Dept — International Organizations** — sparse topical feed; newest 22 May dedups to existing. *Same B1/B2 caveat.*
- **Court of International Trade** — feed 200/99 items, all 99 URLs already in DB (verified read-only via docker); specialized trade court, ~15d quiet is normal. (Most solid C — backed by a DB check.)
- **Australia DFAT** — curl_cffi reproduces discovered=119 exactly; ~2-3 releases/month; newest 2026-05-23 already ingested.
- **Joint Chiefs of Staff** — dnn_articlecs + live API agree: newest 2026-05-20, only 2 posts in ~4 weeks; episodic readout cadence.

**Borderline-C → recommend route to B (dedup, not pure cadence):**
- **State Dept — Collected Department Releases** (lean, `active:false`, byte-identical duplicate of active Press Releases feed). Real issue = YAML-vs-DB `active` drift: `feed_freshness.py` ~L107 filters `WHERE s.active = true` but the 2026-05-12 prune only flipped YAML, leaving the DB row active. **Reconcile the DB `active` flag** (don't re-enable the duplicate). Same drift likely affects other pruned sources — audit.

---

## Category D — Inconclusive / bot-blocked / parked (read-only could not determine; needs a next probe)

- **MIIT News** — JS-rendered list; needs the TRS-WCM JSON list endpoint (all probed candidates 404'd) or a new playwright China handler. Next probe: capture the browser network tab for the `comp_5790085` list-data XHR. (Also in B3.)
- **People's Daily Politics** — hard 403; needs a playwright attempt + a working 200 politics-listing path that carries `n1` links. (Also in B4.)
- **China Electricity Council** — TCP-level block from the mini's IP; can only be settled from an in-China egress. (Also in B5.)
- **Zhejiang Government** — `active:false`; `www.zj.gov.cn` returns HTTP 405 + WAF "已阻断" even under curl_cffi (sibling Shanghai works, so site-specific). Keep off OR playwright + specific `/col/colNNN/index.html` news column. Watchdog NEVER flag is a false positive on a disabled source.
- **State Dept soft-block determinism** — the block is **intermittent** (Africa: blocked then 3/3 clean; Europe: clean under UA). Next probe must run from the **actual containerized scheduled-run vantage** with body-dump logging to confirm whether prod is hit, since local probes don't reproduce reliably.

**Pruned-but-flagged (SCRAPER_NOT_RUNNING — watchdog false positives on disabled sources, verification skipped):**
- **Beijing Government** (`active:false`, china_sources.yaml L392) — live site fresh (newest 2026-06-01); re-enabling viable but is an operator decision; if re-enabled, narrow the over-broad link_pattern (currently also matches ~80 nav/index pages).
- **CSRC** (`active:false`, china_sources.yaml L298) — pruned for PBOC/SAFE overlap; stale DB `active=true` row is why the watchdog flags it; link_pattern also latently broken (absolute vs root-relative hrefs). Reconcile DB `active`.

**Systemic watchdog fix implied by D:** disabled sources (Beijing, CSRC, Zhejiang, Collected Releases) shouldn't be flagged NEVER at all — the watchdog reads stale DB `active=true` rows after YAML-only prunes. Reconcile the DB `active` flag (or make the watchdog read YAML), which clears 4 false positives at once.

### Highest-leverage actions, ranked
1. **Fix the lean extraction phase (B2/DOJ)** — one bug likely unfreezes most lean flags (BLS, CFPB, ARPA-E, all State Dept, CIT share the 24d signature).
2. **Apply the 3 verified Category-A YAML fixes** (SPC pattern, Xinhua Leaders multi-URL, ORNL url) — zero-risk wins.
3. **Reconcile DB `active` flags** for pruned sources — clears 4 watchdog false positives (Beijing, CSRC, Zhejiang, Collected Releases) at once.
4. **Ignore-list the 7 genuine low-cadence sources** in the feedfreshness plist.

Relevant files: `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/config/china_sources.yaml`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/config/sources.yaml`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/config/akamai_sources.yaml`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/scraper/china_scrape.py`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/scraper/discovery.py`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/scraper/extractor.py`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/pipeline/main.py`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/scripts/feed_freshness.py`, `/Users/maxgoshay/code/briefernewsapp/launchd/news.briefer.feedfreshness.plist`.
